BIO 2026: Forget dual track—biopharma deals are more complex than ever
snippet: Dual and even triple or quadruple track processes have come roaring back in 2026 thanks to a glut of M&A that has refilled investors’ wallets. Big Pharma is being put on notice that time is critical if they want to acquire.

title: BIO 2026: FDA’s IND initiative is a step toward normalization after slipshod year
snippet: Policymaking at FDA has been anything but business as usual under the Trump administration, but former regulators cite the agency’s new investigational new drug pilot program as a sign of normalcy.

title: The Golden Triangle and the gaps: Rethinking UK biotech
snippet: In this episode of Denatured, as part of our series on the European life sciences investment ecosystem, you’ll hear from Hakan Goker, managing director at M Ventures and Maina Bhaman, partner at Sofinnova Partners. We explore the UK biotech ecosystem: from the Golden Triangle’s evolving role to the challenges of scali...

title: Revolution’s second RAS blocker doubles chemo response rate in pancreatic cancer
snippet: Encouraging data for combination regimens of Revolution Medicines’ zoldonrasib “reinforce the path to leadership in PDAC” for the biotech, according to Truist Securities.

title: Ionis secures market expansion for Tryngolza as FDA clears severe hypertriglyceridemia use
snippet: The approval of Ionis Pharmaceuticals’ Tryngolza for severe hypertriglyceridemia could spur “substantial growth” for the product, according to William Blair.

title: Gilead’s TROP-2 Trodelvy, Keytruda combo win first-line FDA nods for breast cancer
snippet: Gilead’s Trodelvy can now be used as a monotherapy and in combination with Merck's Keytruda to treat certain patients with triple-negative breast cancer.

title: FDA’s uniQure, REGENXBIO reversals could bolster other near-term rare disease applications
snippet: The FDA’s recently altered outlook on the evidence required for approval of rare disease drugs could have immediate benefits for companies including Skyhawk Therapeutics, Capricor Therapeutics and Biohaven.

title: 10 companies hiring in San Francisco, South San Francisco (plus a few in Brisbane, San Rafael)
snippet: Looking for a new role in and around San Francisco? Check out these companies hiring across all disciplines.

title: Labs see rising demand for nitrosamine, topical performance and microbiome testing
snippet: Recent BioSpace industry conference conversations point to sponsors pushing earlier on safety, quality and performance data, leading to demand for nitrosamine analysis, IVRT/IVPT and microbiome database capabilities.

title: Cursor Deal Puts US On Track For Record Startup M&A Year

title: ADC Therapeutics lays off 17% of global workforce
snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

title: Jubilant draws FDA rebuke for delayed mold investigation and quality unit lapses
snippet: Jubilant HollisterStier received a warning letter over problems that the FDA said necessitate an immediate and comprehensive assessment of the contract manufacturing organization’s global operations.

title: ADC Therapeutics lays off staff after 27 patient deaths mar Zynlonta readout
snippet: After 27 deaths were reported in a group receiving its blood cancer drug, ADC Therapeutics is downsizing, implementing layoffs for about 30 workers.

title: REGENXBIO bets on FDA flexibility with filing plans for Duchenne gene therapy
snippet: A few short days after announcing an FDA pivot on a separate asset, REGENXBIO is planning to test the agency’s apparent newfound rare disease outlook on another late-stage gene therapy.

title: BIO CEO Crowley, DOD doctor Overton are top candidates for FDA commissioner: RBC
snippet: A new report from RBC Capital Markets lists Biotechnology Innovation Organization chief executive John Crowley and military physician Heidi Overton as potential candidates for the role of FDA commissioner—in addition to the agency’s current acting leader, top food regulator Kyle Diamantas.

title: FDA hiring 2,200 people to staff up after last year’s DOGE cuts
snippet: The FDA has so far secured 600 new hires and is looking for 1,600 more as interim leadership at the agency aims to rebuild the workforce and morale after more than a year of intense attrition.

title: Novartis, Antares stars align in cancer deal that could top $1.9B
snippet: Novartis and Antares Therapeutics are shooting for the stars, launching a joint mission to develop small molecule oncology therapies.

title: Lilly deepens relationship with China’s Abbisko for $1.9B biobucks
snippet: Lilly has already spent more than $25 billion in potential business development commitments this year, including the $6.3 billion buyout of Centessa Pharmaceuticals that closed today.

title: AbbVie keeps M&A rolling, FDA’s reversal train chugs along, BIO 2026 dispatch, more
snippet: AbbVie scooped up immunology player Apogee Therapeutics for nearly $11 billion in one of the year’s top deals to-date, while Sanofi made a big play to survive its upcoming Dupixent patent cliff; FDA uncertainty continues as the agency changes direction on gene therapies by uniQure and REGENXBIO; and Jef Akst and Annal...

title: Serapha takes reverse merger route to Nasdaq with $230M and gene editor from China
snippet: The combined business entity with Boundless Bio, which will carry Serapha Bio’s name and fold in Boundless Bio, will focus on the development of a gene editor for alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency.

title: Takeda enters transitional year as new CEO seeks return to growth
snippet: For the 2026 fiscal year, Takeda anticipates declines in revenue and profit, highlighting what CEO-elect Julie Kim says is the need for the company to “invest in future growth.”

title: A novel lactylation-related gene signature deciphers the immunosuppressive microenvironment and stratifies precision therapy in colorectal cancer.

title: An immunological panacea for cancer-related cachexia.

title: BIO 2026: Cell and gene therapy sector must break the mold to thrive
snippet: If cell and gene therapy makers are going to achieve their mission to improve patients’ lives, the industry must come together to share information across stakeholders, from regulators to manufacturers to payers.

snippet: The tumor microenvironment (TME) comprises interacting immune, stromal, and metabolic compartments that determine tumor behavior and treatment response. title: Noncanonical NF-κB pathway driven inflammation across multiple cellular compartments identifies NIK as a therapeutic target for inflammatory bowel disease.
snippet: The noncanonical NF-κB pathway, driven by NF-κB-inducing kinase (NIK), plays a central role in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) pathogenesis by regulating immune responses across epithelial, myeloid, and lymphoid compartments. title: Integrated machine learning and molecular dynamics-driven multi-target virtual screening of FDA-approved drugs for drug repurposing in breast cancer.

title: Association between tumor genomic mutations and the risk of PD-1 inhibitor-induced hypophysitis: a retrospective cohort study.
snippet: Immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICIs) can cause immune-related adverse events (irAEs), with hypophysitis being a serious endocrine toxicity that often requires hormone replacement and poses a risk of adrenal crisis. title: Multi-omics analysis of kidney renal cell carcinoma in silico with preliminaryvalidation.

title: Case Report: Effect of setmelanotide treatment in a young patient with acquired hypothalamic obesity followingsepsis and meningoencephalitis with brain abscess.
snippet: Acquired hypothalamic obesity (aHO) is a rare and severe condition caused by injury to hypothalamic centers regulating appetite, energy balance, and neuroendocrine function. title: Advances in oral disease models: a mini-review of developments from 2015 to 2025.
snippet: Oral diseases represent one of the most widespread global health burdens, affecting billions of people worldwide, causing pain, disability, and substantial treatment costs.
